opp.name: Johnson_Matthey_S2C_ANA via EY
opp.next_step: CLaire chasing ont he intro we want them to come to our AI event on October EY going to introduce us Ch no update The immediate next step is to plan and schedule a discovery meeting or RFP session with Johnson Matthey Plc. This includes mapping key stakeholders, identifying decision-makers and project sponsors, and engaging in multi-threaded communication to influence the evaluation process.
opp.ais_why:
* The documented driver is AI itself: the customer is "struggling with procurement/spend management efficiencies" on SAP and has a stated "Need AI-powered digital solutions for indirect procurement."
* The opportunity scope is AI-centric (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) and routed via EY for an S2C/ANA initiative.
* It is very early: the next step is to "plan and schedule a discovery meeting or RFP session" and map decision-makers - the AI need is stated but the evaluation has not started in earnest.

items.quote: Zero buyer calls, zero contact roles, zero activity since creation 29 May 2026
items.source: Avoma discovery (opp + account + attendee-email) returned zero matched calls; Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query returned zero buyer roles

items.quote: EY going to introduce us. Ch no update.
Zero contact roles on opportunity; no confirmed buyer stakeholders
No business problem, pain, metrics, decision criteria, or process documented
items.source: Zero Avoma calls; Salesforce synthesis-map fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, etc.) empty or not present

metrics.narrative: No business problem, quantified impact, or qualitative pain has been articulated. Zero buyer calls and zero stakeholder engagement mean no discovery has occurred. The opportunity exists as a partner referral (EY) but no buyer need, savings target, cost reduction, cycle-time improvement, or operational pain has been captured in any Salesforce field or call transcript. The immediate move is to secure the first buyer conversation through EY and conduct discovery to surface and quantify the pain.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: 'EY going to introduce us', 'Ch no update' (last modified 25 Jun 2026, 1 day ago)
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Zero contact roles, zero buyer calls, and zero evidence of advocacy, internal introductions, or agenda-driving by a buyer-side contact. The Next_Step__c notes 'EY going to introduce us' and 'Ch no update' — EY is the partner channel but no internal Johnson Matthey champion has emerged. The account-level contact list includes senior procurement leaders (Rachael Legg CPO, Stuart Urwin-Mann Global Procurement Director, Matt Atkins Procurement Transformation Director, Jon Boe Procurement Transformation and GBS Lead) but none is confirmed as engaged in this opportunity. Building a champion is the immediate priority once EY secures the first meeting.

sources: SF Competitors__c: Coupa; Oracle; SAP Ariba (opp creation 29 May 2026)
competition.narrative: Coupa, Oracle, and SAP Ariba are listed in the Salesforce Competitors__c field, but no buyer call, next-step note, or task provides context on threat level, evaluation status, or sentiment. With zero buyer engagement it is unknown whether Johnson Matthey is running an active RFP with these vendors, replacing one of them, or simply named them as category leaders in an EY pre-qualification. Coupa is marked primary_competitor but without evidence of their positioning or the buyer's view. The first discovery call must clarify the competitive landscape and whether this is a live multi-vendor evaluation or a sole-source EY referral.

sources: SF Competitors__c lists Coupa, Oracle, SAP Ariba — implies a competitive evaluation or replacement intent, but this is inferred, not confirmed by a buyer
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been identified. Zero buyer calls mean no pain owner has been named and no confirmed vs inferred pain distinction can be made. The product scope (S2C suite + ANA agentic AI) implies a procurement transformation intent, but whether Johnson Matthey is replacing an incumbent (inferred from competitors Coupa, Oracle, SAP Ariba listed in Salesforce), addressing manual process inefficiency, or pursuing AI-led sourcing automation is unconfirmed. The first discovery call must identify and quote the buyer's articulated pain.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and implementation scoping are completely unknown. Zero buyer engagement means no MSA/SOW discussion, no SI/partner role clarification, and no formalized implementation plan. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function is documented. Discovery must uncover whether Johnson Matthey runs a centralized procurement process, requires InfoSec review, and how EY fits (introducer vs implementation partner vs neither).

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Zero contact roles on the opportunity, zero buyer calls, and no Salesforce field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) names a budget authority. The account-level contact list includes Rachael Legg (CPO) and Alastair Judge (Group CFO), but neither is confirmed as engaged in this S2C+ANA scope. The first discovery call must map the approval chain and identify who controls the commercial decision for a £150k procurement transformation investment.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process or approval chain has been mapped. Zero buyer calls and zero stakeholder engagement mean the evaluation sequence (RFI, RFP, shortlist, demos, workshop, commercials, EB sign-off) is unknown. No Salesforce field (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) documents approvals or gates. The call sequence itself is empty. The first discovery call must establish the process, timeline, and approval layers.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or decision factors have been documented. Zero buyer calls mean no use-case validation, test-case session, or workshop has occurred. No Salesforce field (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) carries criteria. The products in scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) are listed but the buyer's fit requirements, must-haves, and evaluation weighting are unknown. Discovery must surface what Johnson Matthey will measure vendors against.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: No champion, zero buyer engagement, and Coupa/Oracle/SAP Ariba listed but unconfirmed; every MEDDPICC element a gap

blocks.read: Going quiet — engagement has dropped off.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: No meetings on record; EY is to introduce Zycus and a discovery meeting still needs to be planned (no update)

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Coupa, Oracle, and SAP Ariba are listed as competitors but with zero buyer engagement their threat level and status are unconfirmed.

bullets.text: No champion, no engagement, no discovery, no EB

blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.
blocks.title: Customer commitment

items: Escalate to EY partner lead to secure Johnson Matthey introduction and first discovery meeting within 7 days. (by 3 Jul)
Run first discovery call with Johnson Matthey procurement transformation leadership to surface pain, map stakeholders, and qualify the opportunity. (by 10 Jul)
Multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder and Procurement Transformation Director to build parallel stakeholder relationships beyond EY channel. (by 10 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS field data. Zero buyer calls mean no AI appetite signal from the buyer. Product scope includes Merlin Intake and ANA agentic AI, suggesting the deal is positioned around AI-led procurement transformation, but buyer excitement is unconfirmed.

items.change: Opportunity created, staged Qualified, amount £150k, close date 31 Dec 2026, products S2C suite + ANA, competitors Coupa/Oracle/Ariba logged
Next_Step__c updated: 'EY going to introduce us', 'Ch no update', discovery meeting planning noted

deal_movement.summary: Opportunity created 29 May 2026 via EY partner channel, immediately staged Qualified, and has remained static for 28 days with no buyer engagement, no field updates beyond creation, and no activity logged. Next_Step__c last modified 25 Jun 2026 (1 day ago) notes 'EY going to introduce us' and 'Ch no update', indicating the introduction has not yet occurred and there is no update from the channel partner.

items.detail: EY introduction promised but not delivered in 28 days. Partner-sourced opportunity with zero buyer access is at risk of stalling indefinitely if EY does not secure the meeting.

Zero stakeholder access and zero contact roles after 28 days. No confirmed buyer engagement means the deal team has no visibility into internal politics, budget ownership, or project sponsorship at Johnson Matthey.

31 Dec 2026 close date (188 days out) on a dark, unengaged opportunity with zero discovery is not credible. Typical enterprise S2C cycle is 12-15 months; closing in 6 months from a standing start with no buyer relationship is unrealistic.

Coupa, Oracle, and SAP Ariba listed as competitors but with zero buyer engagement their positioning, threat level, and Johnson Matthey's evaluation intent are unknown. Competitive landscape is unconfirmed.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Zero buyer engagement, zero contact roles, and no evidence of internal advocacy. EY is the partner channel but the internal Johnson Matthey champion has not emerged.

items.action: Escalate to EY partner lead to secure Johnson Matthey introduction and first discovery meeting within 7 days.
items.trigger: EY introduction promised but not delivered in 28 days since opp creation. Next_Step__c notes 'EY going to introduce us' and 'Ch no update' as of 25 Jun 2026 (1 day ago).
items.expected_effect: Unlock buyer access and move from dark/unengaged to live discovery. Without EY delivering the introduction within 7 days, qualify the opportunity out.

items.action: Run first discovery call with Johnson Matthey procurement transformation leadership to surface pain, map stakeholders, and qualify the opportunity.
items.trigger: Zero buyer engagement in 28 days since opp creation. No business problem, pain, metrics, or stakeholder map documented. Opportunity staged Qualified but discovery has not occurred.
items.expected_effect: Surface and quantify the business problem, identify Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, map the competitive landscape, and validate the S2C + ANA scope. If pain and budget authority cannot be confirmed, disqualify.

items.action: Multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder and Procurement Transformation Director to build parallel stakeholder relationships beyond EY channel.
items.trigger: Zero contact roles on opportunity after 28 days. Account-level contacts include Rachael Legg (CPO), procurement transformation leadership, and GBS lead, but none confirmed as engaged. Single-thread risk.
items.expected_effect: Build redundancy beyond EY introduction, establish direct buyer relationships, and reduce partner-dependency risk. Add confirmed stakeholders as contact roles.

items.action: Position Merlin agentic AI and ANA negotiation intelligence in first discovery call to differentiate vs Coupa/Oracle/Ariba.
items.trigger: Merlin Intake and ANA in scope but AI narrative untested. Competitors Coupa, Oracle, SAP Ariba listed but differentiation wedge not yet delivered to buyer.
items.expected_effect: Test AI appetite, create differentiation vs traditional S2P platforms, and anchor the value proposition on AI-led procurement transformation.

items.action: Validate or reset 31 Dec 2026 close date after first discovery call based on buyer timeline and decision process.
items.trigger: Close date 188 days out (31 Dec 2026) on a dark opportunity with zero discovery is not credible. Typical S2C enterprise cycle is 12-15 months; 6-month close from standing start is unrealistic.
items.expected_effect: Align forecast date with buyer's actual evaluation timeline and decision process, preventing future slippage.

confidence_signals.summary: No confidence signals. Zero buyer engagement, zero contact roles, zero calls, zero completed tasks or events. The opportunity is dark and unqualified.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 May 2026, qualified immediately, 28 days in Qualified stage with zero buyer activity. Close date 31 Dec 2026 is 188 days out. No submission-date series to compute stage velocity. LastActivityDate is null. No field history movement on stage, amount, or next step beyond creation. Zero time-in-stage momentum.
evidence: Opportunity created 29 May 2026 via EY partner channel
Zero buyer-facing activity in 28 days since creation
Next_Step__c notes 'EY going to introduce us' and 'Ch no update' — the introduction has not occurred
Zero OpportunityContactRole records (no buyer stakeholders mapped)
Zero Avoma calls discovered across opp + account + attendee-email discovery
Zero completed Tasks or Events in 90-day window
Forecast category Pipeline on a dark, unengaged partner lead is indefensible
north_star_verdict.headline: Partner-sourced opportunity remains completely dark 28 days post-creation with zero buyer contact, no stakeholder mapping, and no EY introduction materialized. No calls, activities, or engagement evidence—deal exists in CRM only, not in market reality.

flags: £150k S2C + ANA deal in Qualified stage with zero discovery, zero pain identification, and zero stakeholder mapping is not a qualified opportunity. Discovery must occur within 14 days to surface business problem, quantified pain, decision process, and competitive context, or the deal should be disqualified and the forecast cleared.
best_practice_check.summary: Partner-sourced deal with zero buyer access after 28 days. Immediate priorities: (1) escalate to EY to secure the introduction and first buyer meeting within 7 days, (2) multi-thread to procurement transformation leadership (CPO, Procurement Transformation Director, GBS Lead) via EY or direct outreach, (3) run discovery to surface pain, map stakeholders, and qualify the opportunity.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa, Oracle, and SAP Ariba are listed as competitors but with zero buyer engagement their threat level and status are unconfirmed. No call, next-step, or task provides dated sentiment, evaluation context, or wedge. Coupa is treated as primary competitor by default but without evidence. The competitive read is incomplete until discovery clarifies whether Johnson Matthey is running a live RFP, replacing an incumbent, or evaluating Zycus as a sole-source EY referral.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown until discovery. Must surface Johnson Matthey's Coupa experience (pricing, integration complexity, AI gaps), position Merlin agentic AI + ANA negotiation intelligence as differentiation, and prove S2C ease-of-use vs Coupa's heavy customization.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown until discovery. Likely legacy ERP-tied; wedge on modern UI, Merlin AI, and procurement-specific innovation vs Oracle's horizontal platform.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown until discovery. Wedge on Merlin AI-first approach, ANA negotiation agent, and agility vs Ariba's enterprise complexity and cost.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake and Agentic AI (ANA) are in scope but with zero buyer engagement the AI narrative has not been delivered. No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c data. AI positioning is completely untested.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No customer expectations, evaluation criteria, or fit requirements have been documented. Zero buyer engagement means no discovery, demo, or RFP session has occurred to surface what Johnson Matthey expects or values.

hard.ais_why:
* The documented driver is AI itself: the customer is "struggling with procurement/spend management efficiencies" on SAP and has a stated "Need AI-powered digital solutions for indirect procurement."
* The opportunity scope is AI-centric (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) and routed via EY for an S2C/ANA initiative.
* It is very early: the next step is to "plan and schedule a discovery meeting or RFP session" and map decision-makers - the AI need is stated but the evaluation has not started in earnest.
